title: docs/podman.1.md: rename "containers-registries.conf" to "registries.…
body:
…conf"

Since the correct name for the registries man page is the shorter
"registries.conf", correct the reference in the "SEE ALSO" section.

Note that this is a temporary solution since, in order to avoid possible
future clashes with more generic man pages, all of the podman-related
man pages should ideally start with a "containers-" prefix.

Signed-off-by: Robert P. J. Day <rpjday@crashcourse.ca>

Hi @rpjday. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) or [openshift](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`

The correct name is `containers-registries.conf` (see https://github.com/containers/image/blob/master/docs/containers-registries.conf.5.md). This sounds like a bug in the package you are using. I see the very same issue on Fedora 29.
I am closing the PR but we should keep the discussion alive.

I'm doing all this on Fedora 30 branched (fully updated), where the man page file comes from:
```
$ rpm -ql containers-common
/etc/containers
/etc/containers/policy.json
/etc/containers/registries.conf
/etc/containers/registries.d
/etc/containers/registries.d/default.yaml
/etc/containers/storage.conf
/usr/share/containers
/usr/share/containers/mounts.conf
/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
/usr/share/man/man5/containers-storage.conf.5.gz
/usr/share/man/man5/policy.json.5.gz
/usr/share/man/man5/registries.conf.5.gz    <------------
/usr/share/rhel/secrets
/usr/share/rhel/secrets/etc-pki-entitlement
/usr/share/rhel/secrets/rhel7.repo
/usr/share/rhel/secrets/rhsm
/var/lib/atomic/sigstore
$
```
Does this mean the containers-common package has the incorrectly-named file?

> Does this mean the containers-common package has the incorrectly-named file?

Yes. Other files are affected as well, namely:

- containers-registries.d
- containers-registries.conf
- containers-policy.json
- containers-certs.d

@rhatdan @lsm5 PTAL

Thanks a lot for bringing this up!
@vrothberg Just want to verify, the actual file /etc/containers/registries.conf should not be renamed, only the man pages should change from registries.conf(5) to containers-registries.conf(5) right?

> @vrothberg Just want to verify, the actual file /etc/containers/registries.conf should not be renamed, only the man pages should change from registries.conf(5) to containers-registries.conf(5) right?

That's correct. We changed the names of the manpages last year to have a (more or less) consistent namespacing. openSUSE and maybe others are using this already (see https://build.opensuse.org/package/view_file/devel:kubic/libcontainers-common/libcontainers-common.spec?expand=1), so I think this should be fixed in the Fedora package.

I also mentioned in another issue that, even if the man page file is properly named, for instance, "/usr/share/man/man5/containers-storage.conf.5.gz", it will still respond to the shorter "man storage.conf", apparently because that is picked up from the first line of the file:

`.TH "storage.conf" "5" " Container Storage Configuration File" "Dan Walsh" "May 2017"`

suggesting one needs to fix the files to use the fully-prefixed name. At least it seems that way to me.
Ah ... we also changed all the references in other manpages across the projects.

> I also mentioned in another issue that, even if the man page file is properly named, for instance, "/usr/share/man/man5/containers-storage.conf.5.gz", it will still respond to the shorter "man storage.conf", apparently because that is picked up from the first line of the file:

Good catch! This can be fixed by adjusting the header of the storage manpages.
